title: mentha longifolia subsp. dumortieri
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. dumortieri, also known as wild mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. dumortieri include relieving digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and supporting respiratory health. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
